results.title: [论文评述] Robust-U1: Can MLLMs Self-Recover Corrupted Visual Content for Robust Understanding?
results.content:
多模态推理 (Multimodal Reasoning) 以进行鲁棒理解：
在获得恢复图像 $I\_r$ 后，模型被训练来联合分析损坏图像 $I\_c$ 和恢复图像 $I\_r$ 来执行鲁棒的视觉理解。输入被构造为这两个图像与文本查询 $Q$ 交错的序列。训练目标是最大化地面真值答案 $A^$ 的似然：
$$L\_{MLLM} = -E\_{(I\_c, I\_r, Q, A^)} \sum\_{t=1}^L \log P\_\Theta(a\_t^\ | a\_{<t}^, I\_c, I\_r, Q)$$
其中 $a\_t^$ 是目标答案 $A^\$ 的第 $t$ 个 token。

实验结果表明，Robust-U1 在真实世界损坏基准 R-Bench 上取得了最先进的鲁棒性，并在一般 VQA 基准 (包括 MMMB, MMStar 和 RealWorldQA) 上，在对抗性损坏下保持了卓越的性能。分析证实，高质量的视觉恢复直接增强了推理性能，从而确立了自恢复作为实现鲁棒视觉理解的关键机制。

本文的贡献主要体现在三个方面：

Moonlight Logo

Contact 02-6925-6978 E-mail: moonlight@corca.ai

© 2026 Corca, Inc. All rights reserved. [...] Robust-U1 的核心方法包括三个阶段：

监督微调 (Supervised Fine-Tuning, SFT) 以进行初步重建：
该阶段旨在使统一的MLLM具备从损坏输入 $I\_c$ 重建清晰图像 $I\_o$ 的基础能力。模型基于预训练的统一MLLM (如 BAGEL) 构建，并通过一个特定的恢复提示 $P\_{rec}$ (例如，“Recover the clean version of this corrupted image.”) 进行条件化。图像首先被编码成潜在表示 $Z\_c$。训练目标是去噪一个带有噪声的清晰图像潜在表示 $Z\_o(t)$，其中 $Z\_o(t) = (1-t)Z\_o + t\epsilon$ 是在时间步 $t$ 的噪声潜在表示，$\epsilon$ 为高斯噪声。损失函数定义为：
$$L\_{SFT} = E\_{t \sim U(0,1), \epsilon \sim N(0,I)} | \epsilon - \epsilon\_\Theta(Z\_c, Z\_o(t), t, P\_{rec}) |2$$
其中 $\epsilon\Theta$ 是由模型参数 $\Theta$ 参数化的噪声预测网络。

通过强化学习 (Reinforcement Learning, RL) 提升视觉质量：
为了进一步提高恢复图像 $I\_r$ 的质量，使其在结构和语义上与原始清晰图像 $I\_o$ 更加一致，本阶段采用 Flow-GRPO 强化学习算法，并引入双重奖励机制： [...] Moonlight Logo
Moonlight Logo
Moonlight Logo

# [论文评述] Robust-U1: Can MLLMs Self-Recover Corrupted Visual Content for Robust Understanding?

多模态大语言模型 (MLLM) 在视觉理解方面取得了显著成功，但在真实世界的视觉损坏下，其性能会大幅下降。现有增强鲁棒性的方法存在局限：黑盒特征对齐缺乏可解释性，而白盒基于文本的推理无法恢复像素级细节。为解决这一根本性问题，即MLLM能否自行恢复损坏的视觉内容，本文提出了 Robust-U1 框架，赋予MLLM显式的视觉自恢复能力，以实现鲁棒理解。

Robust-U1 的核心方法包括三个阶段：

results.title: Snapmaker U1 3d Printer Announced Sept 2025 - General - MakeWithTech
results.content:
I ended up buying a Snapmaker U1 and have had it since March and now have probably close to 300 hours on it. It hasn’t been flawless and some of the issues maybe user created. I have been very happy with it and it prints really well, very little waste at all. I still haven’t emptied the poop catch tray but it’s getting close. The only real waste is the prime tower. So far I have printed PLA, PETG, & TPU without issues. Been using the Snapmaker Orca slicer and have no complaints. [...] Looking forward to a long happy printing life.

Right now I’m getting an error about the filament is broken or stuck, only happening on tool head 1. I can press resume and it will continue for it bit then throw the error again. Haven’t troubleshooted it yet. Think I’ll bypass the feeder and see what happens. Hopefully that will give me a hint as to where to look.

Haven’t had that problem, but please report your findings when you figure it out.

### Related topics [...] I am using the PAX12 Extended Firmware which fixes the camera and also adds features such as RFID from OpenSpool, Snapmaker has been constantly updating the firmware and slicer. I see people in the forums having issues but I’m not experiencing any of them. I have had any dealing with SM CS but from what I hear they are pretty easy to work with solving the problems.

I recently got a U1 also and have been mostly happy with it. I had a problem with it “going out of bounds” under certain conditions, but entered a ticket and got rapid response (even though it was a weekend) and got it resolved in a couple of days. The reason was due to time zone differences in getting emails back and forth etc. In the end, I was extremely pleased with the tech support.

results.title: Known Issues and Updates | Department of Health Care Policy and Financing
results.content:
Some professional claims for Continuous Glucose Monitors (CGMs) with a Date of Service (DOS) on or after 11/1/2025 may have paid at an unexpected rate when billed with procedure codes A4238, A4239, A9277, A9276, A9277 or E2103, with no modifier or with a modifier of U1, U2 or U3.

    Affected claims were reprocessed 1/9/26.

    Issue resolved 1/7/26.

    Resolved 11/5/25: Professional Claims for Some Supply Services were Denying for EOB 3958

    Professional claims for Supply services with procedure codes A4238 and A4239 and a Date of Service (DOS) of 10/31/25 were denying for Explanation of Benefits (EOB) 3958 – “NO REIMB RULE FOR PROC.”

    Affected claims will be reprocessed.

    Issue resolved 11/5/25. [...] Some professional claims for Continuous Glucose Monitors (CGMs) with a Date of Service (DOS) on or after 11/1/2025 may have paid at an unexpected rate when billed with procedure codes A4238, A4239, A9277, A9276, A9277 or E2103, with no modifier or with a modifier of U1, U2 or U3.

    Affected claims were reprocessed 1/9/26.

    Issue resolved 1/7/26.

    Resolved 11/19/25: Administrative Codes on Some Professional Claims for the RSV Vaccine were Denying Incorrectly

    Some professional claims with procedure code 90382 and a Date of Service (DOS) on or after 7/1/25 were denying for Explanation of Benefits (EOB) 2861 – “No Rate on File for the Date(s) of Service.” [...] Some professional claims for procedure codes 97153, 97154, 97155 and 97158 with a Date of Service (DOS) on or after 5/1/2024 were denying for Explanation of Benefits (EOB) 0101 – “This is a duplicate service” and EOB 1010 – “This is a duplicate item that was previously processed and paid” when multiple claims are billed by the same provider with the same DOS and with a Place of Service (POS) of 03.

    Issue resolved 4/16/26.

    Providers are encouraged to resubmit affected claims.

Personal Care
:   No Known Issues for this provider type.

Pharmacist
:   Resolved 11/19/25: Administrative Codes on Some Professional Claims for the RSV Vaccine were Denying Incorrectly
